speaker: Petr Pavel
text: Populism in post-communist countries grew from a popular expectation that the 1989 transition would deliver capitalism's benefits while preserving socialism's lack of personal responsibility; when that proved illusory, the resulting frustration was harvested by opposition demagogues blaming elites and institutions.
context: Offered as 'just my perception' rather than thorough analysis; described former communist citizens wanting nice cars, travel, and goods 'but no responsibility.'

grounded_formulations.voice_name: Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
